title: Verification Report Jun 21 — System Insights
summary: An external verifier reviewed all 10 AI-generated proofs and found that every answer is correct, but 4 proofs contain critical logical errors (theorems invoked outside their valid hypotheses) and 4 more contain proof gaps. Only Problems 4 and 10 are fully correct. The system consistently finds the right high-level strategy but fails in load-bearing details.

critical_patterns.title: Theorem misapplication (scope errors)
critical_patterns.description: The system applies theorems to mathematical objects they do not cover. Example: the Godement–Jacquet functional equation was applied to Whittaker functions (Problem 2), but it is only valid for matrix coefficients. Example: equivariant cobordism classes equated with rational classes (Problem 7).

critical_patterns.title: Variable and indexing confusion
critical_patterns.description: Critical variable substitutions are made with the wrong variable. Problem 1: the counterterm uses variable u instead of v in a key step, and the constant c_{N,2} is defined via an expectation that is identically 0 by parity — a sign error in the renormalization group computation.

critical_patterns.title: Proof given at 'Idea' level — load-bearing step unproved
critical_patterns.description: Critical steps are asserted as 'Idea' or 'sketch' without proof. Problem 3: Theorem 2 (the identity G*/P* = F*/P*_κ, the engine of the stationarity argument) is asserted but not proved. Problem 5: the discreteness/suspension claim in the setup is mathematically false. Problem 6: the greedy algorithm base case picks an arbitrary vertex v0 which may violate the boundary-leverage invariant.

critical_patterns.title: Incorrect attribution to proof method
critical_patterns.description: The proof references an incorrect mechanism for a cited theorem. The BGLS convexity theorem was attributed to an exterior-power construction (f^{[k]}) that does not exist in general; the correct argument is a variational supremum of affine functions (Ky Fan generalization). This was fixed in the Problem 4 working_solution.tex on 2026-06-21.

critical_patterns.title: Missing normalizations destroying algebraic properties
critical_patterns.description: Multiple proofs drop a normalization constant that changes the mathematical object. Problem 4 (earlier version): Walsh formula missing 1/n! made the convolution non-monic. Problem 3: reference list truncated mid-entry, removing required citations. These are precision/transcription failures rather than conceptual errors.

recommendations: Add a 'theorem hypothesis audit' step where the critic agent explicitly checks whether each cited theorem's hypotheses are satisfied by the objects it is applied to — especially for theorems with restricted domains (functional equations, equivariant cobordism, renormalization operators).
Require every proof that uses the phrase 'sketch', 'idea', or 'it can be shown' to immediately flag an issue: these phrases signal unproved load-bearing steps that the critic must evaluate.
Add a variable-tracking pass that checks consistency of variable names across multi-step computations (e.g., renormalization expansions where u vs v matter).

For each theorem cited, the solver agent should write one sentence explaining WHY the theorem's hypotheses are satisfied in this specific application — not just cite it. This forces explicit hypothesis checking.
Prioritize Problems 1, 2, 5, and 7 for re-solving: these have critical errors that invalidate specific proof steps, even though the answer is correct. A correct proof path exists for each.

priority_problems.reason: 2 critical errors in counterterm and renormalization constant — fundamental mistakes in QFT renormalization
GJ functional equation applied to wrong object — requires complete replacement of the dual-side argument
Rational vs equivariant class conflation at the key identification — needs a Z/2-equivariant normal cobordism construction
False suspension/discreteness claim in setup — easily fixed by weakening to an inclusion, but invalidates the current proof structure
Theorem 2 unproved — cite branching/symmetrization argument from [1,2] or supply proof

best_proof.problem: Problem 4 — The Finite Free Stam Inequality
best_proof.why_best: The proof has the correct architecture (Blachman strategy via heat flow, score vector, Jacobian contraction) and zero critical errors or gaps. All 5 flagged items were imprecisions (notation, attributions) that have been or were already addressed. The BGLS Step 2 exterior-power claim was fixed on 2026-06-21 to use the correct variational sup-representation argument.

second_best_proof.problem: Problem 10 — Preconditioned CG for RKHS-CP Subproblem
second_best_proof.why_best: Only 1 minor issue total; the proof is essentially complete with correct numerical linear algebra argument.

system_health.note: 100% answer accuracy, 20% proof correctness — the system finds the right answer but often cannot yet produce a rigorous proof
